name: Fastly, Inc.

profile.description: Fastly operates a global edge cloud platform providing content delivery (CDN), security, and compute services. The company has pivoted from a niche CDN into AI-enabling infrastructure, offering Compute@Edge for AI inference at the edge, semantic caching via AI Accelerator, and next-gen WAF security. Full-year 2025 revenue reached $624M, and Q1 2026 revenue of $173M grew 20% YoY with RPO of $369M up 63% YoY.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Fastly has transformed from a niche CDN into an AI-enabling edge cloud platform over the past 3 years, launching AI Accelerator with semantic caching, Compute@Edge for WebAssembly-based serverless compute, and a next-gen WAF security suite that grew revenue 47% YoY in Q1 2026 [1]. This represents a significant business model pivot opening new revenue streams beyond commodity CDN delivery. However, the transformation is still in progress and GAAP profitability has not yet been sustained. AI Deployment (1/2): Fastly's AI Accelerator uses semantic caching to reduce expensive round-trips to centralized GPU clusters, and the traffic classification engine distinguishes between human users, helpful bots, and adversarial AI-driven traffic [2]. These are shipping products, but no specific AI-driven revenue figures or internal cost savings have been disclosed separately. Market Share (1/2): Fastly is a meaningful player in the CDN/edge space but is not a leader — Cloudflare, Akamai, and AWS CloudFront all have larger market share. Forrester named Fastly a Leader in Edge Development Platforms 2026, validating differentiation in the compute-at-edge niche [2]. Product Pipeline (2/2): Specific next-gen products include agentic AI support infrastructure for identity-backed AI agents, enhanced security products, and expanded edge compute capabilities, with 2026 revenue guidance raised to $710M-$725M reflecting 15% growth at midpoint [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): R&D spending is above industry average as a percentage of revenue for a company of Fastly's size, consistent with the platform transformation strategy.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Fastly's mission of empowering developers and businesses to 'build, secure, and deliver digital experiences at unprecedented speed and scale' is clear and identifies what the company does and for whom, but does not articulate a deeper transformative purpose beyond the obvious [3]. It is functional rather than inspiring. Community Impact (1/2): The Fast Forward program provides free services to 100+ open-source projects and nonprofits, demonstrating genuine community investment beyond revenue generation [3]. However, no specific dollar amounts for charitable giving or foundation-level programs were identified. Employee Retention (1/2): The 2023 layoff of 11% of staff was disruptive, but the subsequent delivery of 20+ major software releases in 2025 and smooth CEO transition from Todd Nightingale to Kip Compton suggest the remaining team is executing effectively. No specific tenure or turnover data is available. Insider Buying (1/2): Net insider selling is the pattern — CEO Compton sold $254K in shares, President Lovett sold $332K plus an additional $1.6M in shares in March 2026, and CTO Bergman sold $105K, all in 2026 [4]. No meaningful insider buying was identified. However, sales appear to be routine compensation-related rather than signal-driven.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Fastly's balance sheet is healthy with D/E of 40.7% well below the 70% target, and revenue growth is strong at 19.8% YoY with a 5-year CAGR of approximately 16.5% [1]. However, the company remains GAAP unprofitable with -15.8% profit margin and negative ROE of -0.1%, reflecting ongoing stock-based compensation dilution and operating losses. Forward P/E of 45.4x is elevated, scoring poorly against the 20x target. PEG ratio is unavailable. Liquidity is excellent at $197.6M average daily dollar volume. The improving trajectory — first non-GAAP profitable year in 2025, NRR of 113%, and RPO of $369M growing 63% — suggests the financial profile is inflecting toward profitability.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Fastly is a direct AI beneficiary whose edge cloud platform becomes more valuable as AI workloads proliferate. The physical infrastructure — 353 Tbps global network capacity across dozens of PoPs — cannot be replicated by AI software. AI agents require low-latency edge infrastructure for inference, content delivery for AI-generated media requires CDN capacity, and AI-driven bot traffic increases demand for Fastly's security products (WAF, bot management, DDoS protection) [1][2]. Analysts view AI as a tailwind, not a threat, with management explicitly citing AI as 'an increasing tailwind for the business' in 2026 guidance [1]. However, hyperscaler competition from AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure edge services means Fastly's moat is narrower than pure infrastructure companies — a 7 rather than 8-9. AI Opportunity (1/1): Fastly generates quantifiable AI-related revenue through its AI Accelerator product suite, Compute@Edge platform serving AI inference workloads, and security products protecting against AI-driven bot traffic. Security revenue of $38.8M in Q1 2026 grew 47% YoY, driven in part by AI-related traffic management [1].

infinite_game.summary: Fastly demonstrates meaningful infinite game characteristics through its willingness to fundamentally reinvent its business model from CDN to AI infrastructure, and new CEO Compton brings long-term vision. However, the company faces finite pressures from ongoing GAAP losses, heavy insider selling, and competitive dynamics that force short-term execution focus against larger, better-capitalized rivals.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Fastly's purpose of empowering developers to build faster, safer internet experiences connects its work to the broader mission of making the internet work better for everyone. CEO Compton's emphasis on helping content owners be 'compensated for their content' in the AI era shows values-driven positioning [2]. The cause is genuine and partially lived — the Fast Forward program and developer-first culture reflect it. But it has not been tested through decades of consistency or a major crisis that validated the purpose as the strategic anchor.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The 2023 layoff of 11% of staff was painful but followed by a productive period of 20+ major releases in 2025, suggesting the remaining team is engaged and executing [2]. The CEO transition from Nightingale to Compton was smooth with no reported senior departures. However, the small workforce of 1,140 combined with heavy SBC reliance and recent insider selling creates uncertainty about long-term team stability. Average for the industry.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Fastly explicitly positions itself against Cloudflare, Akamai, and AWS CloudFront by differentiating on its WebAssembly-based architecture rather than denigrating competitors. Management's competitive language focuses on building the best developer-first platform rather than 'beating' specific rivals. The company acknowledges competitors' strengths — Cloudflare's scale, AWS's ecosystem — and uses those as motivation to differentiate through technical excellence [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Fastly's transformation from a niche CDN into an AI infrastructure platform represents genuine strategic flexibility — entering edge compute, AI accelerator products, and security as new business lines while the legacy CDN business faces commoditization [1][2]. The changes are meaningful but incremental rather than transformative — the company extended into adjacent markets rather than abandoning its core technology platform entirely.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Fastly made difficult decisions including the 2023 layoff, CEO change, and investment in AI infrastructure products while still unprofitable [2]. The company maintained R&D investment through losses and set aggressive growth targets rather than retreating to cost-cutting. However, the decisions were arguably forced by competitive necessity rather than proactive courage — the CDN market was commoditizing and Fastly needed to differentiate to survive.

investment_thesis: Fastly is executing a successful pivot from commodity CDN to AI-enabling edge infrastructure, with Q1 2026 revenue of $173M growing 20% YoY, RPO of $369M surging 63% YoY, and NRR improving to 113% [1]. The raised 2026 guidance of $710M-$725M (15% growth at midpoint) reflects management confidence in sustained demand. Security revenue growing 47% YoY to $38.8M demonstrates the platform strategy is diversifying revenue beyond content delivery. The AI Accelerator product suite positions Fastly as critical infrastructure for agentic AI workloads moving to the edge, and the company's first profitable year (non-GAAP) in 2025 signals the business model is inflecting toward sustainability.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on persistent GAAP unprofitability and valuation risk. At 45.4x forward earnings and -15.8% profit margins, the stock prices in a profitability trajectory that has not yet materialized on a GAAP basis [3]. Heavy stock-based compensation dilutes per-share economics significantly. Notable insider selling in 2026 — President Lovett sold $1.6M in shares, CEO Compton sold $255K — raises questions about management conviction at current levels [4]. Competition from better-capitalized hyperscalers (AWS, Google Cloud) and faster-growing Cloudflare creates ongoing pricing pressure in the core CDN business. Customer concentration risk persists, and any deceleration in enterprise AI spending could slow the RPO-to-revenue conversion that underpins the growth thesis.
key_catalysts: Conversion of $369M RPO backlog (up 63% YoY) into recognized revenue through 2026-2027, providing visibility into sustained growth [1]
AI Accelerator and agentic AI infrastructure products driving new enterprise wins as AI inference moves to the edge and security threats from AI-driven bots proliferate
Path to sustained GAAP profitability as operating leverage improves on 15%+ revenue growth against a stabilized cost base post-2023 restructuring
key_risks: Persistent GAAP losses and -15.8% profit margin with no clear timeline to sustained GAAP profitability — heavy SBC dilution depresses real per-share economics [3]
Significant insider selling in 2026 by CEO ($255K), President ($1.9M total), and CTO ($106K) signals potential caution about near-term stock performance [4]

Hyperscaler competition from AWS CloudFront, Google Cloud CDN, and Cloudflare's faster-growing platform creates pricing pressure and customer acquisition challenges in a market where scale economics favor larger players
sources: Fastly Q1 2026 Earnings Release — Record Revenue of $173M, RPO of $369M, May 2026
